In any case, she had been the center of attention since she was young.
These people looked down on her, and she could not be bothered to be associated with them.
However, Fiona was different. She was someone Audrey had once sincerely protected and loved.
Audrey used to believe that sincerity could be exchanged for sincerity, but what she got from Fiona was disappointment again and again.
She had really... had enough.
Their last bit of kinship was gone.
Audrey sneered and reached out to Fiona. She said bluntly, "Mrs. Rhodes. If I remember correctly, I bought every single dress in your wardrobe. If you're so generous, why don't you return them to me in cash now?" Fiona was speechless.
She did not expect Audrey to say such a thing. For a moment, her expression alternated between awkward and angry, which was interesting.
Eliza took a step forward and said firmly, "If you want to settle the score like this, my parents must have spent more money on you all these years. When are you going to pay them back?"
Facing her aggressive attitude, Audrey only chuckled.
She said, "Why don't you let them calculate the profits I've brought to the corporation in the past two years and their investment in me? Do they dare to calculate?"
Even Albert's expression changed slightly when he heard that.
Audrey was indeed the hero who saved Rhodes Enterprises. This debt could not be settled...

"Audrey, at the end of the day, it's wrong for you to wear fake clothes today. I've taught you to be honest since you were young. How can you fake it for the sake of your pride?"
Audrey raised her eyebrows and sneered. "You don't have to worry about whether my dress is real or fake. You better keep your daughter in check first."
Fiona shook her head in disappointment. She wanted to continue educating Audrey when someone suddenly said, "I don't think this dress is fake. What kind of fake looks better than a haute couture?" As soon as these words were spoken, this small area instantly fell into a strange silence.
A moment later, someone muttered softly, "Maybe the imitation is too good. What's the saying? It's a high-grade good among fake."
"In any case, it can't be a dress better than a haute couture dress. The only dress of Koivara that is better than a haute couture dress can only be their unique treasure..."
However, everyone knew Audrey could not afford to wear their unique treasure.
"Now that you mention it, it reminds me that this dress does look a little like Koivara's unique treasure."

Listening to their apologies and compliments, Audrey did not feel very happy.
All she felt was hypocrisy.

###chapter90

Hearing the praise, Audrey did not show any arrogance or conceit.
She was neither servile nor overbearing. She handled the situation freely, making the business big shots admire her even more.
Not many young people could be as humble and low-key as her nowadays. They all thought this girl could achieve great things in the business world.

The children's room was carpeted with a thick, white rug. The curtains on the floor-to-ceiling windows were made of sheer fabric, fluttering gently in the breeze and casting dappled shadows on the carpet.
Sunny sat on the rug, engrossed in assembling a teddy bear made of building blocks that stood half his height. He was focused on his task. silent but not rejecting Audrey's presence as she sat nearby When her request to join him went unanswered, Audrey didn't feel disappointed. She simply kept him company in silence.

Maxwell's mom hung up the phone, her voice cold. "I was just checking in with my uncle at the Ministry of Education. If you don't give me a satisfactory resolution, I'll have someone teach you how to handle this."
Jasper's head throbbed with stress. He forced an ingratiating smile and said, "Please, don't be angry. I will definitely handle this matter well and make sure your children aren't wronged. The resolution will meet your expectations."
Jasper couldn't afford to offend these two, let alone Nathan. Nathan's father was the largest shareholder of the school. If Jasper wanted to keep his job, he couldn't punish his son.
No matter how the conflict started, this time, Jasper would blame everything on Miles. 'After all, he's just a kid from an ordinary family. How dare he offend these rich kids?' Jasper thought.
###chapter373
Thinking this, Jasper shot Miles a glare, then slammed his hand on the table, scolding, "Miles, do you realize your mistake?"
Miles bit his lip hard, his tone filled with stubbornness. "I didn't do anything wrong!"
'There's nothing wrong with me fighting back when others attack me for no reason. Am I just supposed to stand here and take the beating? That doesn't make sense,' Miles thought.
Looking at Miles, who was covered in bruises but still refused to admit his mistake, Jasper's anger only flared up even more.
"Stubborn as a mule, I really think you're hopeless." He angrily pointed at Miles's nose and scolded, "Not only are your grades terrible, but now you can't even behave properly."
Miles gritted his teeth, deciding not to argue back.

"You're expelling me?" Miles asked in disbelief, clenching his fists like a lone, cornered animal, and argued, "They provoked me first, and I was just defending myself. Why should I apologize? I won't accept it!"

Hearing this, Miles slumped his shoulders in frustration. He gritted his teeth and muttered in confusion, "I've never done anything to offend you. Why are you targeting me?"
Suppressing his jealousy, Nathan lowered his voice and leaned in close to his ear, saying," Blame yourself for not knowing your place. You shouldn't have taken the girl I like!"
Hearing this, Miles looked even more puzzled. 'Recently, I've been focused entirely on my studies. When did I ever try to take the girl he likes?' he wondered.
"I don't understand what you're talking about," Miles replied.
Seeing Miles's innocent expression, Nathan's face grew darker.
Nathan ran his fingers through his blonde hair, then coldly said, "Miles, be a man
and own up to your actions. Don't pretend you know nothing!"
Miles was speechless. 'Pretending what?' he wondered.
Seeing him look like an innocent little lamb, Nathan felt even more disgusted. He patted Miles's cheek and said expectantly, "If you don't
want to be expelled, give me mylin
goddess's contact information and promise never to flirt with her again." 
Miles frowned, looking confused. 'I don't even know who Nathan's "goddess" is, so how could I possibly have her contact information?' he wondered.
Seeing that Miles kept playing dumb, Nathan's patience was quickly running out.
Nathan snorted coldly and blatantly threatened, "I've warned you. But if you don't do as I say, I can have you expelled for theft and fighting.
"And I can guarantee no school will take you after that. With exams coming up, you wouldn't want to ruin your future, right?"
Miles glared at him angrily. 'The so-called theft is just their deliberate framing, and the fight was started by them as well. It's not my fault, yet now I'm being threatened,' he thought.
Facing such a scoundrel left Miles
feeling completely helpless. He didn't want to just give in, so he shot
back, "If you like her, then go after her openly. Don't play these Cheap tricks with me!"

going to mess with me? A10
afraid I'll do something to them?"

straight to his face.

Miles shook off Jasper, who was trying to hold him back, his eyes filled with uncontainable rage. He would never allow anyone to insult his family.
At Nathan's startled cry, Miles raised his fist again. Just as his punch was about to land on Nathan's face, a hand suddenly reached out from behind and grabbed Miles's arm.
###chapter374
The moment Audrey pushed the door open and stormed in, a beam of light from outside shone through the door and landed right on her.
She had changed into a comfortable, casual outfit but still had her stage makeup on. Her well-groomed hair gently brushed against her cheek in the light breeze. At that moment, she looked like an enchanting fairy princess straight out of a
fairytale.
Everyone's gaze instinctively fell on her, mesmerized by her beauty.
Upon seeing his sister, Miles was stunned for a moment. After that, the anger in
his chest and the clenched fists quickly dissipated. He lowered his gaze, like a puppy that had been wronged.
Nathan immediately recognized the girl before him as the goddess he had been searching for all week.
His eyes lit up, and without thinking about why his goddess was helping Miles, he eagerly approached Audrey with a submissive smile and greeted her, "Hey, beautiful. We meet again."

A flash of coldness passed through her eyes as she asked in a low, serious voice, "Was it them who cornered you in the restroom and broke your phone?"
Seeing his sister stand up for him immediately, a warm feeling spread in Miles's chest, and he nodded obediently.
After getting a confirmed answer, Audrey finally turned her cold gaze toward Nathan and asked in a low voice, "What did he do to offend you?" Her voice carried an innate sense of authority, honed by years of holding power.

Nathan pointed at Miles's nose, getting more worked up as he spoke. "His family's so poor, and all he's learned is to resort to stealing and shady tricks. You'd better stay away from this kind of person."
Miles immediately shot back, "I didn't steal anything, stop making up lies."
"Still denying it?" Nathan scoffed, crossing his arms confidently. "Do you dare let us search your backpack?"
"Why wouldn't I?" Miles said, tossing his backpack at Nathan.
He believed he had nothing to hide and wasn't afraid of being searched. But the next moment, he watched as Nathan pulled an expensive watch out of his backpack.
Nathan spun the watch around on his finger, then looked at the pale-faced Miles with a smug grin. "Caught red-handed. How do you explain this?"
Miles was stunned for a moment, his mind blank as he said in disbelief, "That's impossible. I've never seen that watch before. I didn't steal it."
"If you didn't steal it, then why is it in your bag?" Nathan sneered, his tone dripping with contempt. "You've got quite the eye, going straight for the most expensive one I own. 
"This watch is worth over ten hundred thousand. If I report this to the police, you're definitely going to jail."
Miles's expression changed slightly, but he forced himself to remain calm and said confidently, "I didn't steal it. There's no way my
fingerprints are on that watch
You're setting me up, and I'm not afraid of you, even if you call the cops."

"What do I want?" Nathan said, putting on a mysterious look, then drawled, "If you get your little beauty to beg me for mercy, I might let you off the hook."
###chapter375

###chapter376

###chapter377

###chapter378

She gazed at Nathan with cold indifference and said each word clearly, "I haven't even settled the score with you for framing Miles."
###chapter379

Fearing that Audrey might hold them responsible, both moms admitted their children's mistakes, and each offered a compensation of 60 thousand dollars.
Audrey accepted it all, planning to use it for Miles' college expenses.
Once the parents took their kids and left, the office quickly quieted down to just four people.
Jasper, feeling uneasy and losing his previous arrogance, desperately hoped Audrey wouldn't target him. He had no money to compensate.
Leo, who initially wanted to support Audrey, felt a bit downcast. But he was more curious about who was helping Audrey and casually probed for information. Audrey brushed it off.

Jasper felt wronged but also relieved. At least he wasn't fired, and he didn't have
to pay damages. This incident taught him a lesson; he wouldn't dare mistreat students again.
###chapter380
After settling these matters, Audrey took Miles to the clinic for medicine. She
originally planned to take Miles for a complete check-up at the hospital. But Miles insisted he was fine, saying it was just minor injuries.
There was only one doctor on duty in the clinic.
Miles sat quietly in the chair as the doctor treated his wounds. His gaze, however, went through the glass and rested on Audrey beside him. He was a bit curious about why Xander seemed so afraid of Audrey.
He knew Audrey was the CEO of Rhodes Enterprises, but Xander was said to be a significant figure with influence everywhere. Why would he fear her?
As Miles considered it, his mind began to wander, and he even started to suspect that Audrey might be like characters in movies-a CEO by day and the leader of a secret organization by night.
Before long, Miles had finished his treatment. The doctor reminded him to keep the wound dry and return daily to change the dressing. Miles noted everything down.
After they walked out of the clinic, Audrey patted Miles on the shoulder and said, "Now that Nathan has transferred to another school, no one should dare to bully you anymore. But if any foolish people try, find me, and I'll help you."
Before Miles could agree, Audrey continued, "Mom was so stressed about what happened to you that she fainted. She's still in the hospital, so if anything happens, call me."
Hearing this, Miles felt really guilty and said gloomily, "It's my fault again, making Mom and Dad worry about me. Audrey, can I go with you to visit Mom in the hospital?"

Simon's expression changed, feeling a foreboding sensation.
The next moment, Oliver walked straight to Sarah and appreciatively said, "Ms. Kim, your student Miles has improved quickly. How did you manage it?
"You should discuss your methods with other teachers to help more students with their final preparations."
Sarah was still in a trance. Her expression was blank as she replied, "Miles from my class? He has always been very smart."
Simon couldn't accept it and held on to one last bit of hope. He said, "Mr. Perry, how many places did Miles move up? Did he get into the top ten in the grade?"
According to their bet, no matter how much Miles improved, it was a loss as long as he didn't get into the top ten.
Oliver was in a good mood and didn't notice Simon's unusual expression, Smiling, he said, was near the bottom in the last
monthly test, but in just a monet
he's climbed into the top five in the grade." 
Hearing this, Simon's face turned pale.
Lost in his thoughts, Oliver continued, "This student has unlimited potential. We must nurture him well; he might even bring glory to our school in the SAT."
###chapter389
Upon hearing this, Simon was dumbfounded, and Sarah looked utterly incredulous.
Sarah, in a daze, said, "Top five in the grade? Did Miles from our class achieve this? There can't be a mistake, right?"
Oliver replied, "It's him. The student ID is correct. He's from class 7."
Sarah couldn't contain her joy. She slapped her thigh in excitement and exclaimed, "I always said Miles has a talent for studying. He didn't focus on his academics before and got a bit lazy, but he didn't disappoint me in the end."
Simon felt heartbroken. His heart raced, his blood boiled, and his breathing quickened. In panic, he exclaimed, "How is that possible?
"No one can improve so much in such a short period. Miles must have cheated on the exam."
Sarah's smile froze and her expression became hesitant.
However, Oliver gave Simon a cold glare and said with a stern face, "I was also the proctor for this month's exam, and Miles happened to be in the room I was monitoring. Are you accusing me of letting things slide on purpose?"
Simon tugged at the corner of his mouth and said, "No, that's not what I meant." His mind raced, and he added, "I'm just worried that Miles might cheat, which wouldn't be fair to the others.
"Even if you monitor the exam, there could be things you might miss, Mr. Perry."
Another chemistry teacher suddenly said, "I can assure you that Miles did not cheat. I invigilated Miles during the chemistry exam. I stood by his desk for quite a while and watched.
"His problem-solving was clear, and he even solved the final question, which was quite difficult. There's no way he could have cheated right under my nose."
Simon was left completely speechless. Thinking about their bet, a wave of dizziness washed over him.
Oliver wasn't about to let Simon off easy. He scolded, "You're Miles's physics teacher. You should know how hard he's been working lately. How can you accuse a student of cheating in public? That's unacceptable."
Simon gritted his teeth and unwillingly said, "Sorry, Mr. Perry. I was anxious for a moment and didn't realize this student had real skills. I must have been blind."
Oliver snorted and handed Simon the report card with a stern expression. He said, "Be more careful in the future! Go post the results; the students are eager to see them."
Simon agreed with a sullen look.
"The results are posted on the bulletin board!" During the break, someone shouted at the classroom door. All students instantly rushed out like a swarm, all heading towards the bulletin board. 
Miles remained seated, absorbed in reading a collection of challenging physics problems.
Miles' seatmate nudged his shoulder and asked curiously, "Miles, why aren't you checking your grades?"
They were seated in the last row of Class 7, facing the back door. With the door open, even students outside could hear their conversation.
Among them was a boy from Class 1, a high achiever who consistently ranked in the top ten of the grade. He overheard Miles' seatmate and mocked, "Does Miles need to check his scores? He's always at the bottom anyway." 
Miles couldn't be bothered to respond and didn't glance his way.
Feeling bored, the student snorted and walked away dismissively.
After everyone left, Miles finally dropped his head onto the desk and sighed in frustration. Even the joy of getting a new phone from Eric this morning couldn't lift his mood.
Miles
Jessica told Miles that the family would be having lunch with Alistair's family. But it was now the final
stretch of senior year. The teacher
often reminded them not to take
leaves, fearing they'd fall behind if they slacked off. 
Miles thought, 'It seems quite impossible to ask for leave. Or maybe I should just sneak out by climbing the fence?'
While lost in thought, Miles suddenly heard urgent footsteps and desks and chairs clashing in the classroom. He looked up and saw a classmate rush over to him.
The classmate's eyes were shining with excitement as he exclaimed, "Miles, top five in the grade, you made it to the top five! Wow, how did you manage that?"
###chapter390
Miles blinked slowly, momentarily lost for words.
Still excited, the classmate caught his breath and said with pride, "You're the top student this time, Miles. You've made our class proud!"
Miles felt a weight lift off his shoulders and smiled in relief.
*****
Over an hour later, Audrey walked down the stairs with a flushed face, trembling slightly.
Sunny was having breakfast in the dining room. Noticing how weak Audrey seemed, he quickly got up to support her arm and guided her to a chair.
His words were a little unclear. His big round eyes were full of worry as he said, "Mommy, sick."
Noticing Sunny's concern, Audrey sheepishly touched the tip of her nose, her ears turning even redder.
Alistair, who was a step behind, saved her from awkwardness by saying, while ruffling Sunny's hair, "Your mommy isn't sick."
Sunny didn't believe it and kept staring at Audrey. He said, "Her face is all red." "Maybe it's just hot," Alistair said with a straight face.
Hearing this, Audrey's mind flashed back to the passionate scene in the closet, and she shot a fierce look at Alistair.
She couldn't figure out what came over her, allowing herself to be so easily tempted into making the first move on Alistair. The outcome was obvious.
She was barely able to handle the situation. She probably wouldn't have made it downstairs for another hour if she hadn't begged for mercy.
Alistair wasn't upset by her glare. Instead, he stepped forward to massage her back, soothing her softly and gently.
Seeing the sweet interaction
between his parents, Sunny, a bit jealous, sulked and pouted. The next moment, he got up and brought the prepared bread and oatmeal to Audrey, thoughtfully picking up a small spoon to feed her. 
Audrey was touched by Sunny's kind gesture. She ate the oatmeal Sunny fed her. Then, she gently cupped Sunny's face and affectionately tousled his hair. Her eyes sparkled as she said, "Sunny is awesome; I love you the most!" 
Sunny squinted with a bright smile, his expression cheerful and sunny. No one who saw this scene would believe he was a child struggling with severe bipolar disorder and autism.
Audrey kissed his forehead as a reward.
Sunny immediately raised an eyebrow at Alistair with a smug glint that was hard to miss in his eyes.
Although Alistair felt slightly jealous, he knew Sunny never forgot a slight. Since he was in a good mood today, he decided not to argue.
After they finished breakfast, Alistair went to his study for a meeting.
Audrey took Sunny's hand and went to the closet to pick out clothes for the family gathering later that afternoon.
Inside the closet, Sunny sat quietly on a small chair, swinging his legs. He watched Audrey as she picked clothes from the closet.
Audrey looked up and down. After a long time, she picked out a mint-green children's outfit with cute little horns on the hood. She held up the clothes in front of Sunny and softly asked, "Sunny, how about you wear this today? 
"This is the new outfit Grandma bought for you last time. You haven't worn such a
bright color before; you'll look great."
Seeing Audrey's hopeful expression, Sunny nodded in agreement.
Audrey immediately helped him put it on.
The clothes fit just right, and the mint-green color softened Sunny's serious nature, making him look like a cute bundle of joy.
Audrey couldn't hide her amazement. She kissed Sunny on the cheek and exclaimed, "Sunny, you're the cutest kid in the world. I love you so much."
###chapter391
Sunny was thrilled. He couldn't wait to dash to the study, like a proud little
peacock, showing off his new clothes to Alistair.
Alistair, annoyed by Sunny's boastful behavior, promptly shooed Sunny out of the study.
The dinner was scheduled for the afternoon, still five or six hours away.
Audrey thought about the bracelet she received from Evelyn and decided it was time to return the favor.
So, while Alistair was busy with work, Audrey took Sunny out shopping.
But once outside, Audrey couldn't help but feel a bit troubled. She asked, "Sunny, do you know what Grandma likes?"
Sunny blinked slowly as if he was deep in thought. He thought, 'Grandma likes so many things. How should I say it?'
After thinking for a while, Sunny raised his wrist with his smartwatch, tapped the screen, and showed Audrey some elegant vases, antique paintings, and calligraphy collections.
Audrey caught on. She said, "Do you mean Grandma likes collecting these antiques?"
Sunny's eyes lit up. He nodded eagerly as if saying, "Mom, you are so smart."
Audrey laughed. She raised her hand triumphantly like she was ready to charge forward and exclaimed, "Then I know where to go to buy gifts. Let's go!"
Twenty minutes later, Audrey and Sunny arrived at the biggest antique market in town.
Sunny was dressed in new clothes, while Audrey casually wore some laid-back attire. She looked like she was passing by for some window shopping, not explicitly coming to a treasure hunt at the antique market.
That was why most of the vendors on the antique street didn't pay attention to them, and sometimes, a few would give them disdainful looks.
Audrey held Sunny's hand and calmly walked through the various stares. None of these shops had what she was searching for.
Choosing antiques was all about which one caught one's eye. Audrey wasn't sure what she wanted, so she went for what she liked at first glance.
Audrey hadn't chosen anything as they were about to reach the end of the antique street. Then, they came to a quiet shop around the corner.
The shopkeeper was lounging comfortably on a small chair at the entrance, eyes half-closed, enjoying the sun. He didn't get up when he heard footsteps.
He casually said, "If you're interested, feel free to come in and take a look. It's just
a small business, but it's completely honest."
He was the only shop owner they met along the way who didn't judge them by their looks first.
Audrey led Sunny into the shop.
As soon as they stepped inside, the shopkeeper followed them in. He was waving a fan and wearing a navy blue tank top. He looked quite young As soon as Alex saw Audrey
and Sunny, he begating
them. 
He said, "I should've known when those magpies wouldn't stop chattering in front of my house this morning. The visit was from an angel and a celestial child. With such grace, I'll give you a 20% discount on whatever you choose today" 
Hearing the shopkeeper's playful words, Audrey couldn't help but chuckle. She felt she had chosen the right store.
With a cheerful grin, the shopkeeper asked, "What are you looking to buy, my little angel?"
Audrey looked around the store. She softly replied, "I need a gift for the elders at home. Would you mind recommending something for me?"
"Of course I can. It's my honor to assist you," the shopkeeper replied with a smile. Then he quickly led Audrey to some items on the shelf.
He introduced, "Take a look at this crystal decoration, grand and entirely made of pink crystal, beautiful and impressive. Do you like it?"
Audrey took a close look and gently shook her head. She said, "I haven't seen how the elder's home is decorated, so I'm not sure if it would fit."
"Well, I see," the shopkeeper replied. He eagerly recommended several other antiques.
Audrey still felt something wasn't quite right until she noticed that
Sunny's gaze was fixed on
something. Following his gaze, Audrey saw an exquisitely beautiful screen. 
###chapter392
The screen was made of elaborately carved wood with rose embroidery. It had six panels, stood about six feet tall, and was around ten feet wide. The embroidery was handmade, with tiny stitches and tastefully dyed colors.
Audrey gently patted Sunny's head and softly asked, "Sunny, do you like this screen?"
Sunny kept his eyes on the screen but shook his head.
Audrey paused, confused for a moment. Then, a sudden thought came to her. She softly asked, "Does Grandma like it?"
Sunny nodded.
Audrey was thrilled. After looking closer at the screen and feeling it was nice and presentable, she asked the shop owner, "How much is this screen?"
The shop owner squinted his eyes. He smiled and said, "If you're interested, I'll give you a reasonable price: 93 thousand."
The price seemed reasonable, not too outrageous. Audrey immediately decided to buy it.
Just as Audrey was about to pay, a stunningly attractive woman suddenly walked in from outside. Standing against the light, the woman pointed arrogantly at the screen and said to the shop owner, "I want this screen."
Noticing that the woman who came in completely ignored her, Audrey felt slightly displeased. She spoke up, "Excuse me, lady, I saw this screen first."
The woman removed her large sunglasses from the bridge of her nose, looking Audrey up and down with a gradually disdainful expression.
The woman thought, 'A poor person dares to compete with me. What an overestimation of herself.' She sneered slightly, smirked, and arrogantly said, "So what if you saw it first? I can pay first and even offer double the price."
Then she pulled out her card and handed it to the store owner, saying, "This card doesn't require a PIN, just swipe away."
Audrey frowned deeply.
The store owner didn't take the woman's card. Instead, he calmly said, "I'm sorry, but this screen was reserved by this lady first. Even if she hasn't paid yet, it's still hers."
He added inside, 'Besides, it's not like she isn't going to pay; she was just about to when you interrupted. In business, people have to follow the first-come, first- served rule.'
The woman's face went rigid. She glared at the store owner and asked, "Are you crazy? Don't you want to make money?"
The store owner gave a faint smile and replied, "I only make the money I'm entitled to make."
Audrey raised an eyebrow slightly, thinking, 'This store owner has quite the character.'
The woman was furious. She glared at the store owner angrily and whispered, "Ungrateful fool, do you know who my husband is? It's your good luck that I fancy something from your shop!"
"No, I don't," the store owner replied calmly, showing the same calmness as if standing firm in a storm.
The woman took a deep breath and
said arrogantly, "You blind fool, my husband is Zachary Oxman, the boss of the antique street. Before you offend me for an ordinary customer, consider your own position." 
The store owner blinked in surprise, his expression changing slightly.
Audrey guessed this Zachary probably had quite an influential background. She thought the store owner would cave to the pressure and sell the screen to the
woman.
Unexpectedly, the shop owner stood firm on the principle of first-come, first-served. He firmly stated, "Mrs. Oxman, even if Mr. Oxman himself came today, I wouldn't break the store's rules." en 
He gently wayed a fan in his hand. His tone stayed calm but carried an unquestionable authority as he said, "This young lady first selected this screen A gentleman doesn't spoil others' preferences. I'm sure Mr.
Oman would understand."
Zachary's wife, Lily, was speechless. Having dominated the antique street for so many years, she had never encountered a shop owner so out of touch with reality.
She thought, 'He is so stubborn. No wonder his business is the worst on the entire street'
###chapter393
Audrey seized the opportunity and quickly took out her phone to pay by scanning the code. She admired the shop owner's style so much that she intentionally rounded up the payment.
The shop owner beamed joyfully. He completely changed his cold demeanor and began showering Audrey with praises.
Seeing she was ignored, Lily was so furious she almost fainted. Pointing at the owner in rage, she said, "I'll remember this today, you better watch out in the future."
The shop owner seemed oblivious to Lily's threats. He still politely said, "Take care, Mrs. Oxman."
Lily, still harboring a belly full of harsh words, was furious. With no way to deal with the shop owner, she could only grit her teeth and mutter about making him pay in the future, then angrily turned around to leave.
But when she turned, whether she didn't see Sunny next to her or did it on purpose, she bumped hard into him.
Sunny had a dazed look on his face ever since Lily appeared. There was no time to dodge before the collision, and he ended up sitting on the ground with a thud.
Seeing Sunny knocked down, Audrey scolded with a stern face, "Stop right there! How can you walk like that and not even realize you hit a kid."
Before she could finish her sentence, Sunny's sharp and painful scream suddenly echoed in her ears.
Sunny sat on the ground. He held his head tightly with both hands and cried helplessly, "Don't come near me, don't hit me!" He wished he could curl up into a small ball. His voice was sharp and filled with despair.
Audrey felt like her heart was being stabbed as she hugged Sunny tightly.
But Lily, who had upset Sunny, had a look of disdain on her face. She patted her coat and remarked nastily, "Brat, what are you screaming about? No manners!" She even spit on the ground before strutting away.
Audrey couldn't afford to argue with Lily. She just held Sunny close, softly comforting him with a loving expression. She said, "Sunny, my dear, don't be scared. I am here. No one will hurt you ever again."
Her voice was gentle, but Sunny seemed even more agitated for some reason. He thrashed and screamed like a little animal cornered.
Audrey felt her heart twisting in pain.
The commotion attracted several tourists, and a crowd gathered at the store entrance, all craning their necks to look inside.
Audrey could only try her best to shield Sunny with her body.
Luckily, the store owner saw Sunny wasn't feeling well and quickly closed the door.
Only then did Audrey feel relieved. She gently comforted Sunny and skillfully massaged him, relaxing his tight muscles to prevent his body from stiffening.
Gradually, the crying weakened. Sunny's emotional outbursts were finally released, making him able to listen.
Audrey gently touched Sunny's tear-streaked face and softly said, "Sunny, don't be scared. No one's going to hurt you, really not."
Sunny grabbed her wrist tightly, like a drowning person holding onto a lifeline.
Audrey's face was full of concern. Sunny must have been provoked into losing control, but she wasn't sure what set him off.
She thought, 'Is it Lily's rudeness or the curious eyes of the onlookers? He's afraid of being hit. Could it be that Lily bumped into him too roughly, triggering some ba memories? 
'But the whole Hayes family spoils Sunny like he's their most precious treasure.
So logically, no one would dare to even touch him.'
Unable to figure it out, Audrey put her doubts aside. She thanked the shop owner and took Sunny home. As for the screen she bought, Audrey asked the shop owner to help deliver it home. 
Once Audrey got home, she found that even though Sunny wasn't screaming and crying anymore, he had become clingy. Whenever she left him even for a moment, he'd panic and immediately look for her. 
If he couldn't find her in a second, his eyes would fill with tears, and he'd look as if
he were about to cry.
